_comment: Shared behavioral contract for text-pattern matching in the node-selector resolvers. ONE source of truth for expected behavior, consumed by BOTH implementations' test suites: Kotlin `TrailblazeNodeSelectorResolver.matchesPattern` (via MatcherParityFixturesTest in trailblaze-models jvmTest) and the TS mirror `resolver.ts` (via matcher-parity.test.ts). Every case runs through the REAL resolver in BOTH dialects: `nativeMatches` is asserted via native selector shapes (androidAccessibility), `maestroMatches` via Maestro shapes (androidMaestro), which carry Maestro's Orchestra semantics (IGNORE_CASE | DOT_MATCHES_ALL | MULTILINE + toRegexSafe invalid-regex escape). If either implementation drifts, that side's suite fails. Add a case here whenever matching semantics change - never encode new semantics in only one language's tests.

cases.name: exact literal text matches
full-string match, not substring: 'ok' does not match 'book'
regex semantics apply when the pattern matches as a regex
digit classes and bounded quantifiers
alternation
regex matching is case-sensitive by default
explicit anchors are permitted inside the pattern
valid-but-unmatchable currency pattern rescued by literal fallback
literal fallback requires exact equality, not similarity
literal fallback is full-string, not substring
zero-amount currency literal
currency with thousands separator
long natural-language value with parens and dollar sign
literal fallback is case-sensitive (valid regex miss)
invalid regex falls back to literal equality
literal fallback is case-sensitive (compile failure)
corpus: leading + is not a valid quantifier target, literal rescue
corpus: modifier row with (+$1.00), literal rescue
corpus: un-interpolated template placeholder self-matches literally
Java quote section \Q...\E matches its literal content
quote section does not match different content
regex parts outside a quote section keep regex meaning
unterminated \Q quotes to end of pattern (Java semantics)
escaped backslash before Q is not a quote start
quote-section pattern also literal-matches its own raw source
inline (?i) flag: case-insensitive match
inline (?s) flag: dot matches newline
combined (?is) flags
without (?s), dot does not cross newlines
empty pattern matches empty text
empty pattern does not match non-empty text
non-ASCII text matches literally
symbols and emoji match literally
maestro dialect is case-insensitive by default
leading (?-i) forces case-sensitivity (matching case)
leading (?-i) forces case-sensitivity (wrong case rejected in both dialects)
native opts into case-insensitivity with leading (?i)
maestro dotall+multiline: inner anchors match per line, dot crosses newlines
full-input matching stays absolute under the m flag
inline (?m) alone does not turn full-input matching into per-line matching
maestro invalid-regex literal rescue is case-insensitive (toRegexSafe)
scoped inline group (?i:...) is honored
combined leading (?s-i): dotall added, case-sensitivity restored (matching case)
combined leading (?s-i): wrong case rejected in both dialects
unsupported leading flag (?x) is dropped harmlessly for plain patterns
